items.problem: (configureProcessGroup) Cancel returns non-ESRCH kill errors directly to exec.CommandContext, which ignores cancel errors and proceeds to Wait; the kill error is discarded and never reaches the caller, so a failed group kill (e.g. EPERM from a privilege-dropped child) is silent and indistinguishable from a clean kill

Timeout path returns res, nil unconditionally when context is done, silently discarding runErr — if the process-group Cancel fails (e.g. EPERM from setuid grandchildren or LSM restrictions), the kill is incomplete and orphans survive without any signal

positions.problem: Timeout path returns res, nil unconditionally when context is done, silently discarding runErr — if the process-group Cancel fails (e.g. EPERM from setuid grandchildren or LSM restrictions), the kill is incomplete and orphans survive without any signal

items.problem: (configureProcessGroup) The group SIGKILL fires only when runCtx.Done() triggers Cancel; if the command exits 0 or non-zero before the timeout, Cancel never fires and any orphaned grandchildren survive indefinitely

ESRCH is matched with == rather than errors.Is - correct today because syscall.Kill returns a bare Errno, but a future wrapped return would misreport the already-exited race as a hard kill error instead of ProcessDone

positions.problem: ESRCH is matched with == rather than errors.Is - correct today because syscall.Kill returns a bare Errno, but a future wrapped return would misreport the already-exited race as a hard kill error instead of ProcessDone

items.problem: (TestRunConfiguredValidation_TimeoutReapsGrandchild) processAlive uses bare PID without any uniqueness guarantee across the 5s Eventually window — OS PID recycling can flip the assertion to a false-negative failure or, if a new process happens to appear at that PID, a false-positive pass

The happy-path test only covers exit-0; AC2 requires pass/fail runs to be unaffected, but no test verifies that a clean non-zero exit preserves ExitCode and sets Passed()=false under the new cancel override

positions.problem: The happy-path test only covers exit-0; AC2 requires pass/fail runs to be unaffected, but no test verifies that a clean non-zero exit preserves ExitCode and sets Passed()=false under the new cancel override

items.problem: If the context is cancelled before cmd.Start() finishes, cmd.Process is nil. The override returns nil, but this race condition is untested and could mask start errors or interact unpredictably with future Go versions that call Cancel differently

positions.problem: If the context is cancelled before cmd.Start() finishes, cmd.Process is nil. The override returns nil, but this race condition is untested and could mask start errors or interact unpredictably with future Go versions that call Cancel differently

items.problem: Non-ESRCH errors from syscall.Kill (e.g., EPERM if a group member drops privileges) propagate up through cmd.Run() and bypass the TimedOut branch in RunConfiguredValidation, masking a failed reap as a clean timeout

positions.problem: Non-ESRCH errors from syscall.Kill (e.g., EPERM if a group member drops privileges) propagate up through cmd.Run() and bypass the TimedOut branch in RunConfiguredValidation, masking a failed reap as a clean timeout

items.problem: 250ms timeout races with shell scheduling under CI load: sh may be preempted before echo $! executes, leaving res.Stdout empty and causing require.NotEmpty to fail before the Eventually reaping assertion can run
